Hey, what's going on everyone? I wasn't sure the best place to put this because this is a strategic concept, but it's something that we added on to our sales process that was an innovation from the community, from our general work community, and it's just incredibly powerful. And so it has been coined the no-sale sale, how to turn nos into cash, but it's basically being able to make money from the people who are otherwise saying no. Now before I dig into this, I want to make sure that I am 100% clear. You should have absolute conviction and you should not sell soft because you know you have this in your back pocket. So that's what I want to be clear about. You should still have conviction that your service, in addition to supplementation, nutrition, etc., is better than not having service and just having supplementation nutrition, right? And at home workouts, right? So if you're working out with a trainer at a gym, you're going to get better results than if you're not. And you should have that conviction as should your salespeople. So I want to be very clear about that, that you need to have that conviction going into the sale and not think, well, if they say no, I can do this. Okay, so I want to be very, very upfront and blunt about it. You will help people more if you can sell them into service. Now that being said, it's a numbers game and there's always going to be some people who are afraid of making decisions, whose preferred method of purchase is going to be more geared towards product and less towards service and that's because of their own beliefs, etc., right? Or because they don't think that they're going to be able to show up or if it's busy, whatever it is, it doesn't matter. The point is that some people are still going to say no to service and that is where you pull out the no-sale sale. So what is the no-sale sale? This is the old sales process, which is fine, right? You get 100 leads, 60 scheduled, 30 show, 15 close. Average numbers, it's a 15% close rate of leads, right? And that's at a $500, $600 price point, so you're making between $7,500 and $9,000, right? So that's great. But what if the 15 people who said no, you could reschedule them to come in and start the program and have half of them show and close five? And so essentially, you would take your close from 15 closes to 20 closes because you'd have an additional seven sales appointments from these people. So how does this work? So the prospect comes in, right? And you explain the deposit money back offer, you explain the money back guarantee, and they still say no, right? So then and only at that point, which is why I'm always in favor of having smaller groups and or 101 exclusively because it allows you to have more flexibility in the sale so that you can come up with unique solutions. So that's why for us, like at Argems, we always scheduled two people for 30 minutes because we just knew that one of them would show. And that's how we how we did things. So I just want to be clear about that. Now, once that person says no, you can then offer them the ability to do the program that you just sold them on for no money down. Okay, they're going to think that you are the coolest person ever. And it should give you a lot of peace of mind and peace of heart that you really are helping people in another way, right? You're really truly offering help to every person that you possibly can and meeting them at their level. All right. Now, you're going to offer them for $0 down because you're going to service them from home. Now, what does that mean? You're not going to go to their house. It means that they're going to get an online program. Now, the portals that we've provided for you for each of the plays have at home workouts that are already built into it so that those people can work out at home. Okay. Here's where it gets even cooler. So you'll offer them that and then what you're going to do is you're going to schedule their start time orientation. And so part of the sale here is just saying, listen, I still want to help you. I still totally want to help you solve the problem that you came in to solve. So we're going to give you everything that I just told you about. The meal plans, the grocery list, the preparation instructions, eating out guides, everything. And you're going to do the fitness. The only component is that you're just going to be doing it at home instead of doing it here with a trainer. Does that sound good? Okay, cool. So I want you to still feel like you're part of the community. I still want you to feel like you're starting with a group. And so we're going to schedule for orientation time on Saturday, just like everyone else, and you're going to come in and we're going to weigh you in and make your meal plans and everything. Okay. So they're going to feel like you're really going above and beyond what you are. Key caveat here is that you should separate the times of the no sale sales from the customers who are paying deposits. Okay. Just for sake of not confusing people, it's just easier. Otherwise, just trust me on that one. Just have them at separate orientation times, which you should do anyways to get more personalized service. All right. So schedule them separately for their nutritional orientation. All right. Now once they come in for the nutrition orientation and at that point, you will use the supplement selling script to sell them supplements while giving them that home on my version. Now the show rate is the same as the normal consultation show rate, which is about half of them will show. So if you have 14 people who said no during the week, schedule all 14, about seven will show. The good news is that with supplement sales, you'll actually have a much, much higher close rate than with service sales. And that's because people prefer to buy product, especially people who say no. Why? Because usually it's because they value physical products more than they value service. And so ironically, we've had plenty of people who say, I can't afford this, blah, blah, blah, and then come back, turn around and spend $400, $500, $600 on supplements two days later. It's happened so many times, it's not even worth making a story about it. It's just the truth, it's how it works. And so what's cool about this is that now we're making money from people who said no to service, but yes to products. And so we have now essentially turned nos into yeses. And so it allows you to recapture about half of the people who say no into sales. And what's even cooler about this, so here's a little note for the fulfillment side because this is the next natural question, is that all you have to do is check in with them weekly. So still put them on the clipboards like you normally would, which you'll get to in the next part of fulfillment here in this program. You'll still put them on the clipboards, you'll understand what I say, what I mean that there is you put all the customers on the clipboards, just separate them underneath as another list. And you just check in with them like you normally would, right? It doesn't take you a lot of time to do that. And then at the halfway point, you'll have them commit. And normally where we would sell the abandoned hope sale, you're going to do the exact same sale, except you're going to be selling them into the membership. And so right now we have an absurdly high conversion rate for people who come in for the halfway point consultation. And if for some reason you don't succeed in selling them the service, because at that point usually they're like, you know what, because they trust you because at this point you've built up value, you've provided all this stuff for essentially nothing because they're not going to see product as something that they're really paying you for. It's going to be clear here. Most consumers don't see it that way. So you're going to attempt to sell them the continuity for the membership at this point. And if they say no, then still just reinforce the continuity sale for the supplements. And so you can make, and then at that point if they want to choose to continue still getting checked in on and all this stuff, they just have to agree to continue to purchase supplements. So you're still fulfilling that first six weeks totally okay. And then after that point, if they want to continue and you can choose to deliver them service provided they stay on the supplement continuity. So that's kind of the deal. Now how does this work out? Financially it costs you about $15 an hour for a trainer. And if you check with them once a month, then it's a 30 minute meeting and you're doing one tax check in per month on the other 14 days. So it's like 14 days tax check in, 14 days meeting. Very straightforward. For 30 minutes it's going to cost you $7.50. That's your hard cost. With payroll costs it might be $9. But whatever. Point is though, from that you're going to be able to generate $80 plus per month in net revenue. So they have to be on a bundle of four bangers which is $200 a month or more or higher in order to be able to qualify for us checking in with them and providing them all this stuff. This is on the back end after you've fulfilled the original six weeks. And so just like that you have another revenue stream that comes in that doesn't take any square footage. Doesn't require, it doesn't decrease your capacity in any way. And it's a new revenue stream that literally did not exist before from people who were going to say no and walk out the door. And that is the value of the no sale sale. And that is why continuing to innovate the sales process is so powerful. So that is the no sale sale. In the fulfillment section I'll explain how to do the supplement sale in a little bit more detail. And it will be borrowed directly from the supplement selling secrets training. So if you've already gone through that in its entirety and you already understand how that works. If you have not then just make sure to watch the few videos that are necessary there so that you understand how the supplement sale works. And either way you're going to have to register as a distributor so that we can make sure you actually get paid. So that's the point. So I hope that makes sense. That is the no sale sale in a nutshell. And yeah, I hope that excites you as much as it excites us to make it for you.
